body: This PR cleanly extracts ~500 lines of GOTO pipeline code from StrataMain into proper library modules and converts 68 files to Lean 4 modules. The extraction is well-motivated (fixing interpreter failures in E2E tests) and the module conversion follows a consistent pattern. The proof rework in ProgramWF.lean is an improvement — it removes a `sorry` and uses `grind` effectively. The `panic!` → `throw` change in `emitProcWithLifted` is a good behavioral improvement. The `#eval!` → `#eval` changes are appropriate given the module boundary constraints. The expected file changes are purely byte-offset shifts with identical test outcomes. Two minor items below, neither blocking.

comments.body: Nit: This file imports `Strata.Languages.Core.Identifiers` twice — once as `public import` (line 10) and once as `public meta import` (line 11). Is the `meta` variant needed in addition to the regular one, or is this a leftover from iterating on the conversion?

The old code silently fell back to `.Integer` on type conversion failure. The new code adds `dbg_trace` warnings, which is an improvement for debuggability. Just noting this is a deliberate behavioral change (not just a code move) — the `dbg_trace` output will appear in the Lean server log during evaluation.
